evals.prompt: Find decaying content on example.com, separate ranking losses from seasonality, and save detailed refresh briefs in the default location.
evals.expected_output: Runs the bounded DataForSEO MCP workflow, calculates reproducible position and ETV changes, classifies sampled declines, parses no more than five priority pages, and writes the dated Markdown report under SEO/example.com/.
assertions: Historical Rank Overview, Relevant Pages, Ranked Keywords, Historical SERPs, Historical Keyword Data, Google Trends, and OnPage Content Parsing are used in sequence.
Historical SERPs are limited to ten keywords with no more than two per page unless the user approves more.
Structural, Seasonal, Mixed, and Inconclusive/stable classifications follow the documented thresholds.

evals.prompt: Tell me which old articles have lost organic traffic and what to update.
evals.expected_output: Asks for the missing domain and waits without making a billable DataForSEO call or writing a report.
assertions: The workflow asks for a domain and waits.
No DataForSEO call is made before a valid domain is supplied.

evals.prompt: Analyze https://www.example.org/blog for content decay in Germany using de and save the report under ./client-reports.
evals.expected_output: Normalizes the project domain, uses the supplied market and report root consistently, distinguishes demand decline from position decline, and produces page-level refresh decisions and briefs.
assertions: The project domain is example.org while ranked page URLs remain exact normalized URLs.
Germany and de are used consistently across compatible MCP calls.
Traffic impact uses DataForSEO ETV deltas rather than an invented CTR curve.
Each actionable page receives an updated outline and internal-link recommendations.
